volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 3.3.887 - Section 4.1.958b)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 3.3.887
sections.original: Si quid faciendum est mulieri male atque malitiose ,
sections.translation: If a woman has anything to do mischievously and maliciously , in that case her memory is immortal at remembering it for everlasting ; but if anything is to be done for a good purpose , or honestly ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.890
sections.translation: it will fall out that those same women will become oblivious that instant , and be unable to remember .

sections.label: Section 3.3.891
sections.original: Ergo istuc metuo , quom venit vobis faciundum utrumque :
sections.translation: Therefore do I fear that same , because both those things happen to be about to be done by us ; for that will be a benefit to me in which you both will be acting mischievously towards the Captain .

sections.label: Section 3.3.893
sections.original: Dum nescientes quod bonum faciamus , ne formida .
sections.translation: So long as we do anything that ’ s good , not knowing it , don ’ t you fear . No woman is awkward ⋯ Have no apprehensions , they are ready for the worst .

sections.label: Section 3.3.896
sections.translation: So it befits you . Do you follow me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.896b
sections.translation: Why do I hesitate to go and accost them ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.897
sections.original: venire salvom gaudeo , lepide hercle ornatus incedis .
sections.translation: I am glad that you have come safe . I ’ faith , you bring them nicely dressed .

sections.label: Section 3.3.898
sections.original: Bene opportuneque obviam es , Palaestrio . em tibi adsunt
sections.translation: Well met , and opportunely , Palaestrio . See , here they are whom you commissioned me to bring , and in the very dress .

sections.label: Section 3.3.899b
sections.translation: Well done : accept my thanks .

sections.label: Section 3.3.900
sections.translation: Palaestrio salutes Acroteleutium .

sections.label: Section 3.3.900b
sections.translation: Prithee , who ’ s this , that calls me so familiarly by name ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.901b
sections.translation: This is our master-plotter .

sections.label: Section 3.3.902
sections.translation: Health to you , master-plotter .

sections.label: Section 3.3.902b
sections.original: Salva sis . sed dic mihi , ecquid hic te
sections.translation: And health to you . But , tell me , has he any way given you full instructions ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.903b
sections.translation: I bring them both thoroughly prepared .

sections.label: Section 3.3.904
sections.original: Audire cupio , quem ad modum ; ne quid peccetis paveo .
sections.translation: I ’ d like to hear how . I ’ m afraid lest you should be making some mistake .

sections.label: Section 3.3.905
sections.original: Ad tua praecepta de meo nihil his novom adposivi .
sections.translation: I have added to your instructions nothing new of my own .

sections.label: Section 3.3.906
sections.original: Nempe lúdificari militem tuom erum vis ?
sections.translation: I suppose you wish the Captain , your master , to be gulled .

sections.label: Section 3.3.906b
sections.translation: You ’ ve said what ’ s true .

sections.label: Section 3.3.907
sections.original: Lepide et sapienter , commode et facete res paratast .
sections.translation: Cleverly and skilfully , adroitly and pleasantly , the whole thing is planned .

sections.label: Section 3.3.908
sections.original: Atque huius uxorem volo te esse adsimulare .
sections.translation: In fact , I wish you to pretend to be his wife .

sections.label: Section 3.3.908b
sections.translation: That shall be done .

sections.label: Section 3.3.909
sections.translation: To pretend as though you had set your affection on the Captain .

sections.label: Section 3.3.909b
sections.translation: And so it shall be .

sections.label: Section 3.3.910
sections.original: Quasique ea res per me interpretem et tuam ancillam ei curetur .
sections.translation: And as though this affair is managed through me , as the go-between , and your servant-maid .

sections.label: Section 3.3.911
sections.original: Bonus vátes poteras esse , nam quae sunt futura dicis .
sections.translation: You might have made a good prophet ; for you tell what is to be .

sections.label: Section 3.3.912
sections.original: Quasique anulum hunc ancillula tua abs té detulerit ad me ,
sections.translation: As though this maid of yours had conveyed from you this ring to me , which I was then to deliver to the Captain , in your name .

sections.label: Section 3.3.913b
sections.translation: You say what ’ s true .

sections.label: Section 3.3.914
sections.original: Quid istís nunc memoratis opust quae commeminere ?
sections.translation: What need is there to mention these things now , which they remember so well ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.914b
sections.translation: Still , it is better .

sections.label: Section 3.3.915
sections.original: nam , mi patrone , hoc cogitato , ubi probus est architectus ,
sections.translation: For think of this , my patron ; when the shipwright is skilful , if he has once laid down the keel exact to its lines , ’ tis easy to build the ship , when ⋯ Now this keel of ours has been skilfully laid and firmly placed ; the workmen and the master-builders are not unskilled in this business .

sections.label: Section 3.3.920
sections.original: si non nos materiarius remoratur , quod opus qui det
sections.translation: If he who furnishes the timber [*] does not retard us in giving what is needed , I know the adroitness of our ingenuity — soon will the ship be got ready .

sections.label: Section 3.3.922
sections.original: Nempe tú novisti militem meum erum ?
sections.translation: You know the Captain , my master , then ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.922b
sections.translation: ’ Tis strange you should ask me . How could I not know that scorn of the public , that swaggering , frizzle-headed , perfumed debauchee ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.924b
sections.translation: But does he know you ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.924c
sections.translation: He never saw me : how , then , should he know who I am ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.925b
sections.translation: ’ Tis most excellent what you say . For that reason , i ’ faith , the thing will be able to be managed all the more cleverly .

sections.label: Section 3.3.926b
sections.translation: Can you only find me the man , and then be easy as to the rest ? If I don ’ t make a fool of the fellow , do you lay all the blame on me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.928b
sections.translation: Well , go you in then ; apply yourselves to this business with all your skill .

sections.label: Section 3.3.929b
sections.translation: Trust me for that [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.3.930
sections.original: Age , Periplectomene , has nunciam duc intro ; ego ad forum illum
sections.translation: Come , Periplecomenus , do you conduct them at once in-doors . I ’ m off to the Forum ; I ’ ll meet him , and give him this ring , and will tell him that it has been delivered to me from your wife , and that she is dying for him . As soon as we shall have come from the Forum , do you send her ( points to MILPHIDIPPA ) to our house as though she were privately sent to him .

sections.label: Section 3.3.934b
sections.translation: We ’ ll do so ; trust us for that .

sections.label: Section 3.3.935
sections.original: Vos modo curate , ego illúm probe iam oneratum huc acciebo . —
sections.translation: Do you only attend to the business ; I ’ ll now polish him off with a pretty burden on his back .

sections.label: Section 3.3.936
sections.original: Bene ambula , bene rem geras . egone hoc si efficiam plane ,
sections.translation: Go , with good luck to you , manage the matter cleverly . ( To ACROTELEUTIUM .) But now , if I shall manage this adroitly , that my guest can this day gain the mistress of the Captain , and carry her off hence to Athens ; if , I say , this day we shall succeed in this plan ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.939_940
sections.translation: what shall I give you for a present ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.939_940b
sections.translation: ⋯ If now the lady seconds our efforts on her part ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.941
sections.translation: I think it will be right cleverly and adroitly managed .

sections.label: Section 3.3.941b
sections.translation: When a comparison shall be made of our artifices , I have no fear that I shall not prove superior in the cleverness of my contrivances .

sections.label: Section 3.3.944
sections.original: Abeamus ergo intro , haec uti meditemur cogitate ,
sections.translation: Let ’ s go in-doors , then , that we may deeply weigh these plans ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.945
sections.original: ut accurate et commode hoc quod agendumst exsequamur ,
sections.translation: that carefully and cautiously we may carry out what is to be done , so that , when the Captain comes , there may be no tripping .

sections.label: Section 3.3.946b
sections.translation: You are delaying us with your talk .

sections.label: Section 4.1.947
sections.original: Vólup est , quod agas , si id procedit lepide at que ex sententia ;
sections.translation: ’ Tis a pleasure what you do , if it succeeds agreably and to your mind . For I this day have sent my Parasite to King Seleucus , to lead those soldiers , that I have levied , hence to Seleucus ;

sections.label: Section 4.1.950
sections.original: qui eius regnum tutarentur , mihi dum fieret otium
sections.translation: in order that they may defend his kingdom till I have leisure to attend in person .

sections.label: Section 4.1.951
sections.original: Quin tu tuam rem cura potius quam Seleuci , quae tibi
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you attend to your own concerns rather than those of Seleucus . What a charming new proposal is being offered to you through me as the negotiator .

sections.label: Section 4.1.953
sections.original: Immo omnis res posteriores pono atque operam do tibi .
sections.translation: Well then , I lay all other things aside , and I give my attention to you . Speak out : my ears , in fact , I surrender at your disposal .

sections.label: Section 4.1.955
sections.original: Circumspice dum , ne quis nostro hic auceps sermoni siet .
sections.translation: Look around , then , that no one here may be an eavesdropper for our discourse ; for this business was entrusted me to transact with you in private .

sections.label: Section 4.1.957
sections.translation: ( looks around ). There ’ s no one near .

sections.label: Section 4.1.957b
sections.original: Hunc arrabonem amoris primum a me accipe .
sections.translation: In the first place , receive from me this pledge of affection . ( Gives him the ring .)

sections.label: Section 4.1.958
sections.translation: What ’ s this ? Whence comes it ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.958b
sections.translation: From a charming and a handsome lady , one who loves you , and dotes upon your extreme beauty .
